  "findings": [
    "Backfill lifts exceed the maximum thickness allowed for the compaction equipment in use, preventing proper soil density."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Excavate the improperly compacted backfill down to the pipe bedding zone.",
    "Re-lay and compact the backfill in controlled lifts not exceeding 6 inches (or manufacturer/spec limits) using the appropriate compaction equipment."
  ],
  "rationale": "IPC Section 306 and standard project specifications require backfill to be placed in thin, controlled lifts (typically 6 inches maximum for hand/light compaction) to prevent pipe damage and settlement. Placing lifts that are too thick for the compactor results in inadequate soil density, risking future pipe shear, joint failure, or ground settlement.",
  "references": [
    "IPC 306.2",
    "IPC 306.3",
    "Project Specifications"
  ]
}
